channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's State of the Union. And, House rejects aviation safety bill
item.description: Trump's State of the Union underplayed the economic problems that voters are concerned about. And, the House rejected a bipartisan aviation safety bill after the Pentagon abruptly withdrew support.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's State of the Union underplayed the economic problems that voters are concerned about. And, the House rejected a bipartisan aviation safety bill after the Pentagon abruptly withdrew support.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: When a horse whinnies, there's more than meets the ear
item.description: A new study finds that horse whinnies are made of both a high and a low frequency, generated by different parts of the vocal tract. The two-tone sound may help horses convey more complex information.
item.content:encoded:
A new study finds that horse whinnies are made of both a high and a low frequency, generated by different parts of the vocal tract. The two-tone sound may help horses convey more complex information.

(Image credit: ullstein bild)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Trump's many tariff tools mean consumer prices won't go down, analysts say
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down President Trump's signature tariffs. But the president has other tariff tools, and consumers shouldn't expect cheaper prices anytime soon, economists say.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court struck down President Trump's signature tariffs. But the president has other tariff tools, and consumers shouldn't expect cheaper prices anytime soon, economists say.

(Image credit: Timothy A. Clary)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: Hundreds of American nurses choose Canada over the U.S. under Trump
item.description: More than 1,000 American nurses have successfully applied for licensure in British Columbia since April, a massive increase over prior years.
item.content:encoded:
More than 1,000 American nurses have successfully applied for licensure in British Columbia since April, a massive increase over prior years.

(Image credit: Taylor Pradine)
item.dc:creator: Brett Kelman

item.title: Tax credits for solar panels are available, but the catch is you can't own them
item.description: Rooftop solar installers are steering customers toward leases instead of purchases. Federal tax credits for purchased systems have ended but are still available for leased ones.
item.content:encoded:
Rooftop solar installers are steering customers toward leases instead of purchases. Federal tax credits for purchased systems have ended but are still available for leased ones.

(Image credit: Jeff Brady)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's State of the Union address
item.description: President Trump hit familiar notes on immigration and culture in his speech Tuesday night, but he largely underplayed the economic problems that voters say they are most concerned about.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump hit familiar notes on immigration and culture in his speech Tuesday night, but he largely underplayed the economic problems that voters say they are most concerned about.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: China restricts exports to 40 Japanese entities with ties to military
item.description: China on Tuesday restricted exports to 40 Japanese entities it says are contributing to Japan's "remilitarization," in the latest escalation of tensions with Tokyo.
item.content:encoded:
China on Tuesday restricted exports to 40 Japanese entities it says are contributing to Japan's "remilitarization," in the latest escalation of tensions with Tokyo.

(Image credit: Andy Wong)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Signs, silence, skipping: How Democrats protested Trump's State of the Union
item.description: The pushback comes as Democrats enter a midterm year in which they hope to make gains in the House and Senate.
item.content:encoded:
The pushback comes as Democrats enter a midterm year in which they hope to make gains in the House and Senate.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Read Trump's 2026 State of the Union address
item.description: Read President Trump's State of the Union delivered on Feb. 24.
item.content:encoded:
Read President Trump's State of the Union delivered on Feb. 24.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)

item.title: Trump honors gold medal-winning men's hockey team at State of the Union amid controversy
item.description: The celebration of the men's team comes after FBI Director Kash Patel's trip to the Games in Milan, and the president's comments about the U.S. women's team, have drawn scrutiny.
item.content:encoded:
The celebration of the men's team comes after FBI Director Kash Patel's trip to the Games in Milan, and the president's comments about the U.S. women's team, have drawn scrutiny.

(Image credit: Kenny Holston)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger
